lede: A look at May 18 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Pope John Paul II and Tina Fey, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Cubana de Aviación Flight 0972
events.desc: Cubana de Aviación Flight 972 crashes in Santiago de las Vegas after takeoff from José Martí International Airport in Havana, Cuba, killing 112 of the 113 people on board.

events.title: Mount St. Helens
events.desc: Mount St. Helens erupts in Washington, United States, killing 57 people and causing $3 billion in damage.

events.title: Eli Cohen
events.desc: Israeli spy Eli Cohen is hanged in Damascus, Syria.

events.title: Operation Passage to Freedom
events.desc: Operation Passage to Freedom, the evacuation of 310,000 Vietnamese civilians, soldiers and non-Vietnamese members of the French Army from communist North Vietnam to South Vietnam following the end of the First Indochina War, ends.

events.title: Deportation of the Crimean Tatars
events.desc: Deportation of Crimean Tatars by the Soviet Union.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Selective Service Act of 1917 is passed, giving the President of the United States the power of conscription.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Union forces under Ulysses S. Grant begin the Siege of Vicksburg during the Vicksburg campaign in order to take full control of the Mississippi River.

events.title: 1695 Linfen earthquake
events.desc: The 1695 Linfen earthquake in Shannxi, Qing dynasty causes extreme damage and kills at least 52,000 people.

events.title: Battle of Buir Lake
events.desc: During the Battle of Buyur Lake, General Lan Yu leads a Ming army forward to crush the Mongol hordes of Tögüs Temür, the Khan of Northern Yuan.

events.title: Matins of Bruges
events.desc: Bruges Matins, the nocturnal massacre of the French garrison in Bruges by members of the local Flemish militia.

births.name: Pope John Paul II
births.line: Head of the Catholic Church from 1978 to 2005

births.name: Vincent du Vigneaud
births.line: American biochemist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Bertrand Russell
births.line: British mathematician, historian, and philosopher, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Jacques Santer
births.line: Luxembourger jurist and politician, 22nd Prime Minister of Luxembourg

deaths.text: Rupert, King of the Romans, Count Palatine of the Rhine
Emperor Taizong of Liao, Liao dynasty emperor of China from 927 to 947

observances.text: Christian feast day: Eric IX of Sweden
